_meta.note: Format: {topic, concept, analogy_en, analogy_hi, visual_hint}. Use djb2(topic+concept) % length for deterministic selection.

analogies.analogy_en: Kafka partition is like train coaches: add more coaches, more passengers travel in parallel. Each passenger (message) gets a fixed seat number (offset) that never changes.
analogies.analogy_hi: Kafka partition train ke dibbon jaisa hai: zyaada dibbe = zyaada parallel passengers. Har passenger (message) ko fixed seat number (offset) milta hai jo kabhi nahi badlta.
analogies.visual_hint: Train with numbered coaches, parallel arrows showing messages flowing

analogies.analogy_en: Consumer group is like a cricket team: each player (consumer) covers a different section of the field (partition). Two players never chase the same ball.
analogies.analogy_hi: Consumer group cricket team jaisa hai: har player (consumer) field ka alag hissa (partition) cover karta hai. Do players ek hi ball ke peeche nahi jaate.
analogies.visual_hint: Cricket field with fielder positions mapped to Kafka partitions

analogies.analogy_en: Offset is like your bookmark in a novel. Kafka doesn't know how far you've read. You remember the page number, come back anytime, pick up exactly where you left off.
analogies.analogy_hi: Offset novel mein tera bookmark hai. Kafka ko nahi pata tu kitna padha. Tu page number yaad rakhta hai, kabhi bhi wapas aa, wahan se shuru kar jahan chhoda.
analogies.visual_hint: Open book with bookmark, arrow pointing to exact page

analogies.analogy_en: Kafka replication is like having multiple photocopies of an important government document: if one office burns down, the other offices still have the original.
analogies.analogy_hi: Kafka replication important government document ki multiple photocopies jaisi hai: ek office jal gaya toh dusre offices ke paas original hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Three identical document stacks with fire hitting one, others intact

analogies.analogy_en: acks=all is like a registered post with delivery confirmation from every post office in the chain. acks=1 is like just getting a receipt from the first counter.
analogies.analogy_hi: acks=all ek registered post jaisa hai jisme chain ke har post office se delivery confirmation aati hai. acks=1 sirf pehle counter ki receipt hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Postal chain diagram showing confirmation signals flowing back

analogies.analogy_en: Redis data structures are like different containers in your kitchen: string is a jar, list is a plate with a stack of rotis, set is a fruit basket (no duplicates), sorted set is a cricket scoreboard (ranked).
analogies.analogy_hi: Redis data structures teri kitchen ke containers jaise hain: string ek dabba hai, list rotiyon ka stack wali thaali, set fruit basket (no duplicates), sorted set cricket scoreboard (ranked).
analogies.visual_hint: Kitchen containers labeled with Redis data structure names

analogies.analogy_en: Redis eviction policy is like a dhaba with limited tables. LRU: the guest who sat longest without ordering gets asked to leave. LFU: the guest who ordered fewest times gets removed.
analogies.analogy_hi: Redis eviction policy limited tables wale dhabe jaisi hai. LRU: jo guest sabse zyaada der se bina order kiye baithe hain unhe hatao. LFU: jo guest sabse kam order kiya use hatao.
analogies.visual_hint: Dhaba with occupied tables, one guest being shown the exit

analogies.analogy_en: RDB is like taking a photograph of your whiteboard every hour. AOF is like writing every word as you say it. Photograph can be older; the transcript is always current.
analogies.analogy_hi: RDB teri whiteboard ki har ghante photograph jaisa hai. AOF har word bolte waqt likhne jaisa. Photo purani ho sakti hai; transcript hamesha current hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Whiteboard with camera snapshot vs continuous transcript scroll

analogies.analogy_en: Redis cluster is like splitting a library into multiple rooms by subject. You walk to the right room directly; no central librarian needed.
analogies.analogy_hi: Redis cluster library ko subject ke hisaab se alag rooms mein baantne jaisa hai. Seedha sahi room mein jao; central librarian ki zaroorat nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Library floor plan with subject-labeled rooms and hash slots

analogies.analogy_en: Redis pub-sub is like a radio broadcast: the station (publisher) transmits once; every tuned receiver (subscriber) hears it simultaneously. But if you tune in late, you missed it.
analogies.analogy_hi: Redis pub-sub radio broadcast jaisa hai: station (publisher) ek baar broadcast karta hai; har tuned receiver (subscriber) simultaneously sunata hai. Late tune kiya toh miss kar gaye.
analogies.visual_hint: Radio tower broadcasting to multiple receivers

analogies.analogy_en: Horizontal scaling is like opening more counters at a railway ticket window during rush hour. More counters, same queue, more throughput.
analogies.analogy_hi: Horizontal scaling rush hour mein railway ticket counter pe zyaada counters kholne jaisa hai. Zyaada counters, same queue, zyaada throughput.
analogies.visual_hint: Railway ticket counter expanding from 1 to 5 windows

analogies.analogy_en: Load balancer is like a traffic police officer at a busy Bangalore intersection: she sees which road is empty and waves cars there, regardless of which driver preferred which road.
analogies.analogy_hi: Load balancer busy Bangalore intersection pe traffic police officer jaisa hai: woh dekhti hai kaunsi road khali hai aur cars udhar bhejti hai, driver ki preference se koi matlab nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Traffic intersection with officer directing flow to least-congested road

analogies.analogy_en: Database sharding is like Zomato distributing restaurants to city-specific teams: Mumbai team handles Mumbai restaurants, Bangalore team handles Bangalore. No team has to know about all restaurants.
analogies.analogy_hi: Database sharding Zomato ka city-specific teams mein restaurants baantne jaisa hai: Mumbai team Mumbai restaurants, Bangalore team Bangalore. Kisi team ko saare restaurants ke baare mein jaanna nahi padta.
analogies.visual_hint: Map of India with database shards overlaid on cities

analogies.analogy_en: Consistent hashing is like a circular race track with pit stops. Cars (data) always go to the nearest pit stop (server) clockwise. Add a pit stop: only the next car's route changes.
analogies.analogy_hi: Consistent hashing ek circular race track jaisa hai with pit stops. Cars (data) hamesha nearest pit stop (server) clockwise jaati hain. Ek pit stop add karo: sirf next car ka route badlta hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Circular ring diagram with servers and keys distributed

analogies.analogy_en: CAP theorem is like a Swiggy delivery during heavy rain: you can be consistent (deliver exact order) or available (deliver something quickly), but not both when the network (road) is failing.
analogies.analogy_hi: CAP theorem baarish mein Swiggy delivery jaisa hai: consistent ho sakte ho (exact order deliver karo) ya available (kuch bhi jaldi deliver karo), network (road) fail ho toh dono nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Venn diagram with C, A, P circles, network partition blocking middle

analogies.analogy_en: Message queue is like a IRCTC waiting list: your request is queued, the train (worker) processes it in order, and you don't need to wait at the counter — you go about your day.
analogies.analogy_hi: Message queue IRCTC waiting list jaisa hai: tera request queue mein hai, train (worker) order mein process karta hai, aur tujhe counter pe wait nahi karna — apna kaam kar.
analogies.visual_hint: IRCTC-style waiting list with numbered positions

analogies.analogy_en: CDN is like Amul distributing milk from local dairies instead of shipping from Gujarat every time. The content (milk) is cached close to where people consume it.
analogies.analogy_hi: CDN Amul ka milk local dairies se distribute karne jaisa hai, har baar Gujarat se bhejne ke bajaye. Content (milk) wahan cache hota hai jahan log consume karte hain.
analogies.visual_hint: India map with edge nodes near major cities, origin server far away

analogies.analogy_en: Rate limiting is like a metro gate that allows only one person per beep. Even if 100 people rush at once, the flow is controlled.
analogies.analogy_hi: Rate limiting metro gate jaisa hai jo ek beep pe sirf ek person allow karta hai. Ek baar mein 100 log aayein, flow control rahega.
analogies.visual_hint: Metro turnstile with person tokens flowing through at fixed rate

analogies.analogy_en: Service mesh is like the postal routing network: your letter (request) doesn't need to know the full route. Each post office (proxy) knows the next hop.
analogies.analogy_hi: Service mesh postal routing network jaisa hai: teri letter (request) ko poora route nahi jaanna. Har post office (proxy) next hop jaanta hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Network of post offices with letters routing between them

analogies.analogy_en: Circuit breaker is like the MCB in your home: when the current is too high (errors too many), it trips automatically. Your other appliances stay safe.
analogies.analogy_hi: Circuit breaker ghar ka MCB jaisa hai: current zyaada hua (errors zyaada hue), automatically trip karega. Baaki appliances safe rahenge.
analogies.visual_hint: Home electrical panel with one MCB tripped, protecting other circuits

analogies.analogy_en: SAGA pattern is like planning a wedding: booking venue, catering, music in sequence. If the caterer cancels, you have a compensating step (book another), not a rollback to an unmarried state.
analogies.analogy_hi: SAGA pattern shaadi plan karne jaisa hai: venue, catering, music sequence mein book karo. Caterer cancel kiya toh compensating step hai (doosra book karo), unmistaken state mein rollback nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Wedding planning checklist with compensation arrows for each step

analogies.analogy_en: API gateway is like the reception desk at a large hospital: you tell them your need, they route you to the right department, handle your visitor badge, and log your visit.
analogies.analogy_hi: API gateway bade hospital ki reception desk jaisa hai: apni zaroorat batao, woh sahi department bhejenge, visitor badge handle karenge, aur visit log karenge.
analogies.visual_hint: Hospital reception desk with routing arrows to different departments

analogies.analogy_en: Docker container is like a tiffin box from home: your food (app), container (tiffin), and carrier bag (Docker engine). The food behaves the same whether eaten at home or at the office.
analogies.analogy_hi: Docker container ghar ka tiffin box jaisa hai: tera khana (app), dabba (container), bag (Docker engine). Khana ghar ya office — same taste.
analogies.visual_hint: Tiffin box diagram with layers showing OS, app, dependencies

analogies.analogy_en: Docker layer cache is like making biryani: if you already have cooked rice in the fridge (cached layer), you only cook the meat. No need to boil rice again.
analogies.analogy_hi: Docker layer cache biryani banane jaisa hai: agar fridge mein pakka hua chawal hai (cached layer), sirf meat pakao. Dobara chawal ublane ki zaroorat nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Biryani with layers showing cached and fresh ingredients

analogies.analogy_en: Kubernetes pod is like a college hostel room: roommates (containers) share the room (network namespace) but have separate cupboards (storage volumes). They come and go together.
analogies.analogy_hi: Kubernetes pod college hostel room jaisa hai: roommates (containers) room (network namespace) share karte hain lekin alag almariyan (storage volumes) hain. Saath aate hain, saath jaate hain.
analogies.visual_hint: Hostel room with multiple beds sharing one door (network)

analogies.analogy_en: HorizontalPodAutoscaler is like a call center manager who adds agents when call volume spikes and reduces when quiet. The scaling decision happens automatically.
analogies.analogy_hi: HorizontalPodAutoscaler call center manager jaisa hai jo calls badhne pe agents add karta hai aur quiet hone pe kaam karta hai. Scaling decision automatically hota hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Call center with agents appearing/disappearing based on call queue length

analogies.analogy_en: Kubernetes Service is like a building's front desk: clients don't know which floor the team is on today. They call the front desk, and calls get routed to whoever is available.
analogies.analogy_hi: Kubernetes Service building ki front desk jaisi hai: clients ko nahi pata aaj team kaunsi floor pe hai. Front desk ko call karo, jab bhi available ho route ho jaega.
analogies.visual_hint: Building front desk routing to different floor numbers

analogies.analogy_en: Database index is like the index pages at the back of a textbook: instead of reading all 600 pages to find 'polymorphism', you jump straight to page 412.
analogies.analogy_hi: Database index textbook ke peeche index pages jaisa hai: 'polymorphism' dhundne ke liye 600 pages padhne ki jagah seedha page 412 pe jao.
analogies.visual_hint: Textbook open to index page with arrow pointing to page number

analogies.analogy_en: SQL transaction is like a UPI payment: either the full ₹500 transfers (both debit and credit succeed) or nothing moves. There's no half-transfer state.
analogies.analogy_hi: SQL transaction UPI payment jaisa hai: ya poore ₹500 transfer hote hain (debit aur credit dono succeed) ya kuch nahi. Half-transfer state exist nahi karta.
analogies.visual_hint: UPI payment screen showing atomic debit-credit pair

analogies.analogy_en: SQL JOIN is like merging two Excel sheets using a common column. INNER JOIN: only matching rows. LEFT JOIN: all rows from left sheet, matched data where possible.
analogies.analogy_hi: SQL JOIN do Excel sheets ko common column se merge karna jaisa hai. INNER JOIN: sirf matching rows. LEFT JOIN: left sheet ki sari rows, matching data jahan possible.
analogies.visual_hint: Two Excel sheets overlapping with Venn diagram showing join types

analogies.analogy_en: Database normalization is like separating your grocery list by store: vegetables in one list, dairy in another. No repetition, each item appears in exactly one place.
analogies.analogy_hi: Database normalization grocery list ko store ke hisaab se alag karna jaisa hai: vegetables ek list mein, dairy doosri mein. Koi repetition nahi, har item exactly ek jagah.
analogies.visual_hint: Single grocery list splitting into normalized sub-lists

analogies.analogy_en: Cache-aside is like checking your WhatsApp saved chats first: if the contact is there, you use it; if not, you look up the phonebook (database) and add it to saved chats.
analogies.analogy_hi: Cache-aside pehle WhatsApp saved chats check karne jaisa hai: contact wahan hai toh use karo; nahi hai toh phonebook (database) mein dekho aur saved chats mein add karo.
analogies.visual_hint: Phone contacts showing cache hit/miss flow with database fallback

analogies.analogy_en: Write-through cache is like a bank passbook: every transaction is immediately written in both your digital record (cache) and the bank's ledger (database) simultaneously.
analogies.analogy_hi: Write-through cache bank passbook jaisa hai: har transaction digital record (cache) aur bank ledger (database) mein simultaneously likha jaata hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Bank passbook with dual write arrows to both systems

analogies.analogy_en: Thundering herd is like all your relatives calling the moment your engagement news breaks. The single source (database) gets overwhelmed simultaneously.
analogies.analogy_hi: Thundering herd teri engagement ki khabar phailte hi saare rishtedaaron ka ek saath call karna jaisa hai. Ek source (database) ek saath overwhelmed ho jaata hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Single phone ringing with 50 callers hitting simultaneously

analogies.analogy_en: TTL (Time To Live) is like the expiry date on a milk packet: after that date, the milk (cache data) is considered stale and you fetch fresh milk (query database).
analogies.analogy_hi: TTL milk packet ki expiry date jaisa hai: us date ke baad milk (cache data) stale maana jaata hai aur fresh milk laate hain (database query).
analogies.visual_hint: Milk packet with expiry date, arrow to fresh-milk-from-database

analogies.analogy_en: Two-phase commit is like a marriage ceremony: Phase 1, both families agree (prepare). Phase 2, the saat pheras happen (commit). If one family walks out during Phase 1, no marriage.
analogies.analogy_hi: Two-phase commit shaadi ki tarah hai: Phase 1, dono families agree karein (prepare). Phase 2, saat pheras hoon (commit). Phase 1 mein ek family chali gayi toh shaadi nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Wedding ceremony with two phases labeled, abort arrow from Phase 1

analogies.analogy_en: Eventual consistency is like WhatsApp group messages: your phone shows the message immediately, your friend's phone shows it 2 seconds later — but eventually, both see the same thing.
analogies.analogy_hi: Eventual consistency WhatsApp group messages jaisi hai: tera phone turant message dikhata hai, dost ka 2 seconds baad — lekin eventually dono same dekhte hain.
analogies.visual_hint: Two phones showing message at different times, converging to same state

analogies.analogy_en: Raft consensus is like a democracy with a term-limited president. Only the elected leader (Raft leader) takes orders from clients. Leaders can be re-elected but need a majority vote.
analogies.analogy_hi: Raft consensus term-limited president wali democracy jaisi hai. Sirf elected leader (Raft leader) clients ke orders leta hai. Leaders dobara elect ho sakte hain lekin majority vote chahiye.
analogies.visual_hint: Election diagram with leader and followers, majority quorum highlighted

analogies.analogy_en: Mutex is like the single key to a shared bathroom: only one person (thread) holds it at a time. You wait outside until the key is returned.
analogies.analogy_hi: Mutex shared bathroom ki single key jaisi hai: ek baar mein sirf ek person (thread) rakhta hai. Jab tak key wapas nahi aati bahar wait karo.
analogies.visual_hint: Bathroom door with single key, queue of threads outside

analogies.analogy_en: Deadlock is like two people blocking each other in a narrow lane: you won't move until he moves, he won't move until you move. Nobody moves.
analogies.analogy_hi: Deadlock do logon ka narrow lane mein ek doosre ko block karne jaisa hai: tu tab hategaa jab woh hatega, woh tab hatega jab tu hatega. Koi nahi hata.
analogies.visual_hint: Narrow lane with two figures facing each other, blocked arrows

analogies.analogy_en: Thread pool is like a taxi stand: a fixed number of cabs (threads) wait for passengers (tasks). No new cab is created per passenger — existing cabs cycle through.
analogies.analogy_hi: Thread pool taxi stand jaisa hai: fixed number of cabs (threads) passengers (tasks) ka wait karte hain. Har passenger ke liye nayi cab nahi aati — existing cabs cycle karti hain.
analogies.visual_hint: Taxi stand with fixed slots, tasks queueing and cabs recycling

analogies.analogy_en: TCP is like courier with signature confirmation: you sign, they deliver, they record. UDP is like dropping a pamphlet in the letterbox: fast, no confirmation, some may be missed.
analogies.analogy_hi: TCP signature confirmation wale courier jaisa hai: tu sign karo, deliver hoga, record hoga. UDP letterbox mein pamphlet daalane jaisa hai: fast, koi confirmation nahi, kuch miss ho sakta.
analogies.visual_hint: Two delivery scenarios: signed courier vs untracked pamphlet drop

analogies.analogy_en: HTTP/2 multiplexing is like a multi-lane highway vs a single-lane road: HTTP/1.1 is one car at a time; HTTP/2 is all lanes open simultaneously on the same connection.
analogies.analogy_hi: HTTP/2 multiplexing multi-lane highway vs single-lane road jaisa hai: HTTP/1.1 ek baar mein ek car; HTTP/2 same connection pe saath mein sari lanes open.
analogies.visual_hint: Single-lane vs multi-lane highway comparison with request streams

analogies.analogy_en: Process vs thread: process is a separate household with its own address; thread is a family member in that house. They share the kitchen and bathroom but have separate bedrooms.
analogies.analogy_hi: Process vs thread: process apne address ke saath alag ghar hai; thread us ghar ka family member. Kitchen aur bathroom share karte hain lekin bedrooms alag.
analogies.visual_hint: House diagram with shared kitchen (heap) and separate rooms (stack)

analogies.analogy_en: Virtual memory is like a hotel room key system: each guest (process) thinks they have the presidential suite (full address space). The hotel (OS) maps them to actual rooms dynamically.
analogies.analogy_hi: Virtual memory hotel room key system jaisa hai: har guest (process) sochta hai presidential suite milA hai (full address space). Hotel (OS) dynamically actual rooms map karta hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Hotel floor plan showing virtual vs physical room mapping

analogies.analogy_en: SOLID principles are like cooking rules: S=one chef, one dish (Single Responsibility); O=add new dishes without rewriting the menu (Open/Closed); L=any sous-chef can replace the head chef for the same dish.
analogies.analogy_hi: SOLID principles cooking rules jaisi hain: S=ek chef, ek dish; O=menu rewrite kiye bina nayi dishes add karo; L=koi bhi sous-chef same dish ke liye head chef ko replace kar sake.
analogies.visual_hint: Kitchen with roles mapped to each SOLID principle

analogies.analogy_en: Design patterns are like standard recipes in a restaurant: every cook knows what 'biryani' means. No need to explain from scratch — just say the name.
analogies.analogy_hi: Design patterns restaurant ke standard recipes jaisi hain: har cook jaanta hai 'biryani' kya hota hai. Scratch se explain nahi karna — sirf naam bolo.
analogies.visual_hint: Recipe book with pattern names as dish names

analogies.analogy_en: Idempotent API is like pressing a lift button multiple times: pressing 3rd floor once or ten times gives the same result — the lift goes to 3rd floor. No duplicate trips.
analogies.analogy_hi: Idempotent API lift button baar baar dbaane jaisa hai: 3rd floor ek baar ya das baar dabaao — same result, lift 3rd floor jaegi. Koi duplicate trips nahi.
analogies.visual_hint: Elevator panel with 3rd floor button pressed multiple times, lift arriving once

analogies.analogy_en: Stateless REST is like ordering at a restaurant where the waiter has no memory: every order is complete and self-contained. 'I'll have what I had last time' is not a valid REST request.
analogies.analogy_hi: Stateless REST aisi restaurant mein order karne jaisa hai jahan waiter ko koi memory nahi: har order complete aur self-contained. 'Pichhli baar waala de do' valid REST request nahi hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Waiter with blank notepad, each order self-contained

analogies.analogy_en: Protobuf is like packing luggage with a custom-fit bag vs a generic cardboard box. Custom-fit (Protobuf) is smaller, structured, faster to unpack. JSON is the cardboard box.
analogies.analogy_hi: Protobuf custom-fit bag vs generic cardboard box mein luggage pack karne jaisa hai. Custom-fit (Protobuf) chhota, structured, unpack mein fast. JSON cardboard box hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Side-by-side luggage: tight custom-fit vs bulky cardboard

analogies.analogy_en: JWT is like a visiting card with a wax seal: the card has your details (payload), the seal proves it was issued by a trusted authority (signature). Anyone can read the card; only you can create the seal.
analogies.analogy_hi: JWT wax seal wale visiting card jaisa hai: card pe teri details hain (payload), seal prove karta hai trusted authority ne issue kiya (signature). Koi bhi card padh sakta hai; sirf tu seal bana sakta hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Business card with three sections: header, payload, wax seal

analogies.analogy_en: RabbitMQ exchange is like a post office sorting room: messages come in, sorters (routing keys) decide which mailbox (queue) they go to. Direct, fanout, topic — different sorting strategies.
analogies.analogy_hi: RabbitMQ exchange post office sorting room jaisa hai: messages aate hain, sorters (routing keys) decide karte hain kaunse mailbox (queue) mein jaayenge. Direct, fanout, topic — alag sorting strategies.
analogies.visual_hint: Post office sorting room with routing arrows to different queues

analogies.analogy_en: CDN edge cache is like a neighborhood grocery store vs the central warehouse: you walk 200 meters to the kirana store instead of 20 kilometers to the warehouse. Same product, much less travel.
analogies.analogy_hi: CDN edge cache local kirana store vs central warehouse jaisa hai: 20 km warehouse ki jagah 200 meter kirana store. Same product, bahut kam safar.
analogies.visual_hint: Map showing kirana store (edge) near user vs distant warehouse (origin)

analogies.analogy_en: Spring IoC is like a wedding planner: you don't go find the caterer, photographer, priest yourself. The planner (container) wires them all together and hands you a finished event.
analogies.analogy_hi: Spring IoC wedding planner jaisa hai: tu khud caterer, photographer, pandit dhundhne nahi jaata. Planner (container) sab wire karta hai aur finished event deta hai.
analogies.visual_hint: Wedding planner connecting all vendors, client receives final event

analogies.analogy_en: Spring AOP is like the security camera system in a mall: it records what happens at each shop (method) without the shopkeeper needing to install their own camera. Cross-cutting concern, handled once.
analogies.analogy_hi: Spring AOP mall ke security camera system jaisa hai: har shop (method) pe kya ho raha hai record karta hai bina shopkeeper ke apna camera lagaye. Cross-cutting concern, ek baar handle.
analogies.visual_hint: Mall with one central camera system monitoring all shops

analogies.analogy_en: Two-pointer technique is like two people walking toward each other from opposite ends of a sorted row of people: they meet in the middle when they find their match. O(n) instead of O(n²).
analogies.analogy_hi: Two-pointer technique sorted row ke dono siro se ek doosre ki taraf chalne wale do log jaisi hai: jab match milta hai middle mein milte hain. O(n) instead of O(n²).
analogies.visual_hint: Sorted array with two arrows converging from both ends

analogies.analogy_en: Dynamic programming is like saving your answers in an exam to reuse in other questions. Compute once, store in a table, look up instead of recomputing.
analogies.analogy_hi: Dynamic programming exam mein apne answers save karna aur doosre questions mein reuse karna jaisa hai. Ek baar compute karo, table mein store karo, dobara compute ki jagah lookup karo.
analogies.visual_hint: Exam paper with answer box being copied to multiple later questions

analogies.analogy_en: Sliding window is like reading a newspaper: you hold a fixed-width frame over the page and slide it. Only the newest column enters, the oldest leaves — you don't reread the entire page.
analogies.analogy_hi: Sliding window newspaper padhne jaisa hai: fixed-width frame page ke upar rakhte ho aur slide karte ho. Sirf newest column andar, oldest bahar — poora page dobara nahi padhte.
analogies.visual_hint: Newspaper with sliding frame highlighting a window of text

analogies.analogy_en: BFS is like exploring a city neighborhood by neighborhood: first all streets one block away, then two blocks. DFS is like following one road all the way to a dead end before trying the next.
analogies.analogy_hi: BFS city ko neighborhood by neighborhood explore karna jaisa hai: pehle ek block door ki saari streets, phir do blocks. DFS ek road pe chalte rehna jab tak dead end na mile phir next try.
analogies.visual_hint: City grid with BFS concentric rings vs DFS single path exploration
